message:
test(escape-parser): fuzz harness for KittyImage::parse_apc

Adds 14 tests (8 proptests @ 512 cases each ≈ 4k iterations, 6 unit
regressions) covering KittyImage::parse_apc — a public APC payload
parser at apc.rs:1061 that previously had zero property-test coverage
(verified by grep across escape-parser and frankenterm-core test
trees).

Contract pinned:
- Crash-freedom on arbitrary bytes, printable bytes, and APC-shaped
  byte sequences (structured fuzzing with `G,key=val,...,;payload`).
- `None` on empty input and any byte stream whose first byte isn't
  `G` (exhaustive + proptest coverage).
- Determinism (same bytes → same Option shape).
- Totality on well-formed APC payloads with supported action verbs
  — parser returns Some/None without panicking and the reported
  `verbosity()` accessor is callable on Some.
- Canonical apc.rs regression shapes re-run with varied numeric
  parameters so shrinking can localize any regression.

Hand-rolled regressions cover known boundary conditions that the
randomized strategies may not hit reliably: bare `G`, `G;`, keys
without `=value`, non-UTF-8 bytes in the key section.

Verified: 14/14 tests pass via
  rch exec -- cargo test --manifest-path frankenterm/escape-parser/Cargo.toml --test proptest_kitty_apc
No panics surfaced. Domain: patterns + escape-parser (pane 5).
